content: Comparative analyses of curiosity-driven reinforcement learning methods (ICM vs RND vs hand-designed curious agents) reveal that intrinsic reward signal stability correlates directly with how the agent’s long-term episodic memory organizes encountered novel states, creating a natural selection pressure that favors memory architectures that compress predictable patterns while amplifying under-explored unexpected anomalies. This leads to the testable hypothesis that tuning intrinsic curiosity reward scaling to match the agent’s memory compression ratio will produce more robust open-ended exploration than either fixed-curiosity methods or memory-agnostic curiosity tuning.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
